input.background: Consider a sequence of 25 Bernoulli trials (binomial situation) where each trial results in either success or failure. From the 25 trials, 6 failures and 19 successes result. Let p be the probability of success, and 1-p the probability of failure.
input.question: Find the estimator of p, \hat{p}, which maximizes that particular outcome.

output.answer: The sample density function can be written as $$ g(19)=\binom{25}{19} p^{19}(1-p)^6 $$ The maximum of $$ g(19) $$ occurs when $$ p=\hat{p}=\frac{19}{25} $$ so that $$ g\left(19 \left\lvert\, p=\frac{19}{25}\right.\right) \geq g\left(19 \left\lvert\, p \neq \frac{19}{25}\right.\right) $$ Now $$ \mathrm{g}(19) $$ is the probability or 'likelihood' of 6 failures in a sequence of 25 trials. Select $$ p=\hat{p}=\frac{19}{25} $$ as the probability or likelihood maximum value and, hence, $$ \hat{p} $$ is referred to as the maximum likelihood estimate.
